channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: James Carville calls Trump’s admission about the Iran war a historic presidential misstep
item.description: Democratic strategist James Carville described President Donald Trump’s recent remarks regarding the U.S. war in Iran as potentially the 'greatest' presidential gaffe in history. During a White House ...

item.title: MEA Highlights India’s Democratic Strength, Diversity, and Minority Protection
item.description: At a press briefing in The Netherlands, Sibi George, Secretary (West) of the Ministry of External Affairs, strongly defended India against criticisms regarding press freedom and minority rights. He em...

item.title: Kerala CM-designate VD Satheesan Announces Ministers and Assembly Leaders Ahead of Swearing-In
item.description: Kerala's chief minister-designate V D Satheesan has formally announced the list of ministers who will take oath along with him in the upcoming swearing-in ceremony on Monday. The cabinet includes prom...

item.title: Senate Parliamentarian Blocks $1 Billion White House Security Funding in Reconciliation Bill
item.description: The Senate parliamentarian, Elizabeth MacDonough, has ruled that a $1 billion provision in the Republican reconciliation bill, intended to fund White House and Secret Service security, including upgra...

item.title: FG Kicks Off South-West Engagement On Cooperative Bank Launch
item.description: The Federal Government don start South-West zonal tour to promote the Cooperative Bank of Nigeria. The programme na part of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu's Renewed Hope Agenda, organised by the Federal ...

item.title: North West Former Premier Bushy Maape Passes Away Following Brief Illness
item.description: Bushy Maape, the former Premier of North West, passed away in Johannesburg on Saturday, 17 May 2026, at 2:51 pm after a short illness, surrounded by his family. Current North West Premier Lazarus Mokg...

item.title: Angat Dam water levels drop below minimum operational threshold
item.description: The Angat Dam, a crucial water source for Metro Manila and nearby provinces, has reportedly dropped below its minimum operating level. This development raises concerns about water supply stability for...

item.title: Regine Velasquez captivates audiences with 'Banal Na Aso, Santong Kabayo' on ASAP XP
item.description: On May 17, 2026, Filipino music icon Regine Velasquez delivered a memorable and captivating performance of the beloved song “Banal Na Aso, Santong Kabayo” on the variety show ASAP XP. Known for her po...

item.title: CBSE Reduces Review Fees for Class 12 Answer Sheets and Offers Refunds for Increased Marks
item.description: Amid concerns over the new on-screen marking (OSM) system used for Class 12 evaluations, CBSE has announced a substantial reduction in post-result review fees to alleviate student anxiety. Fees for ob...

item.title: Sarah Vine Reflects on Leadership Contests and Human Nature in UK Politics
item.description: Columnist Sarah Vine provides a pointed commentary on recent developments within the Labour Party, focusing on leadership dynamics and the personalities involved. She describes the political scene as ...

item.title: How to Avoid Checked Bag Fees on Southwest Airlines
item.description: Southwest Airlines, once known for allowing two free checked bags per passenger, introduced changes in 2025 that now charge $45 for the first checked bag and $55 for the second on most fare types. Tra...

item.title: Bangladesh Take Command in Second Test Against Pakistan with Joy's Half-Century and Strong Bowling
item.description: In the second Test between Bangladesh and Pakistan in Sylhet, Bangladesh have taken a commanding position by the end of day two. After a poor first innings showing where opener Mahmudul Hasan Joy was ...

item.title: Eid ul Adha to be observed on May 27 after Zil Hajj moon sighting in Pakistan
item.description: The Central Ruet-e-Hilal Committee of Pakistan has confirmed the sighting of the Zil Hajj moon, declaring that Eid ul Adha will be observed on May 27 this year. The announcement was made by Maulana Ab...

item.title: Bulgaria’s Dara Makes History Winning First Ever Eurovision
item.description: For the very first time, Bulgaria don knack im own crown for the Eurovision Song Contest as Dara, 27-year-old singer, carry the win come home with her energetic performance of 'Bangaranga'. She shock ...

item.title: Ooni of Ife and Olori Mariam welcome new twin sons, second twins in two years
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Ogunwusi, and his wife, Olori Mariam Ajibola, don welcome twin sons, marking the second set of twins for the monarch in just about two years. Both the queen and the babies...

item.title: Cape Town warns of risks to MyCiTi bus expansion due to potential funding reductions
item.description: Cape Town’s MyCiTi bus network, widely recognised as a successful public transport system in South Africa, now faces uncertainty due to proposed national funding reforms. The potential phasing out of ...

item.title: Remembering Kay Kendall: Iconic British Actress of the 1950s
item.description: Kay Kendall was a celebrated British actress whose career, though tragically short, left a lasting impact on 1950s cinema. She gained international recognition with the 1953 film 'Genevieve,' where he...

item.title: Ouaddou: PSL Title Race Staying Open Until Final Day Benefits League
item.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has expressed that it is positive for the Premier Soccer League (PSL) that his team did not secure the league title in their penultimate fixture against Durban C...

item.title: MNLF Commits to Ensuring Peaceful and Lawful BARMM Elections
item.description: COTABATO CITY — The Moro National Liberation Front (MNLF) has assured the public of its commitment to support the Commission on Elections (Comelec) in ensuring that the September 14 parliamentary elec...

item.title: EACOP Project Nears Completion as Final Stages Begin
item.description: The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project dey progress steadily and is now entering its final stages. Dis major infrastructure initiative, which involves Tanzania and Uganda, aims to transpo...
